index_scope: Combined issue-64 evidence bundle. The lake_stage2_* fields and the 'Regenerated 2026-07-13' claim in provenance_note describe the Crypto Lake Stage-2 integrity reports ONLY. The 2026-07-16 user-approved CryptoHFTData probe (a live vendor fetch with an inconclusive replay, generated later under commit 0239d40) is described separately in the chd_probe block; do not attribute its provenance to the Lake dates/commits above.
provenance_note: Local Crypto Lake 2026-04-01 Stage-2 integrity evidence for issue #64. Inputs are the nine Stage-1 raw units pinned (sha256/fingerprint/rows/bytes) inside the preregistration fixture and re-verified by verify_inputs.json. Run 1 = production scripts/run_binance_recon.py --engine auto (Python oracle; perp replay 84,153 s). Run 2 = stage2-native-run (production process_unit path, explicit native override, perp+spot scales 10/100; perp replay ~29 s). stage2_cross_engine_determinism pins run1==run2 on every semantic manifest field incl. published parquet sha256 (full-day cross-engine conformance, 2026-07-12 amendment). No vendor I/O was performed to generate this Lake Stage-2 evidence (all inputs local); no raw market rows appear in any tracked file. The later user-approved CryptoHFTData probe (chd_probe block; aggregated in vendor_calls_total) is the only live vendor fetch recorded in this index. Regenerated 2026-07-13 with content-hash provenance binding (round 21): every Lake Stage-2 report records prereg_sha256 (canonical content hash of the preregistration) and every consumer requires it to match; all Lake Stage-2 reports re-ran under the amendment-5 contract with identical results — all PASS, cross-engine EQUAL (7 units), verdict CERTIFIED, conformance scales 10/100. This 2026-07-13 regeneration claim covers the Lake Stage-2 reports ONLY; the later 2026-07-16 CryptoHFTData probe (chd_probe block) is a live-vendor fetch with an INCONCLUSIVE replay and is NOT part of this all-PASS/cross-engine claim.

vendor_calls_lake_stage2.scope: Crypto Lake Stage-2 integrity evidence generation only — fully offline/local, no vendor I/O

vendor_calls_total.scope: aggregate across ALL tracked issue-64 evidence in this index = Lake Stage-2 (0/0/$0) + the user-approved CryptoHFTData probe (1 request / 19,743,899 B / $0); see the chd_probe block

chd_probe.final_source_decision_detail: Crypto Lake approved on internal certification only; independent validation not executable (CryptoHFTData window unavailable or refused fail-closed); fallback none
chd_probe.note: One bounded, user-approved mid-day hourly object; genuine delta-only data (no snapshot in the hourly partition) so the fail-closed replay refused. Independent parity is procedurally blocked: the preregistered request_bounds.expansion gate requires a CERTIFIED probe replay (which a delta-only hour cannot yield), so obtaining and replaying earlier hours (00..12) needs a reviewed preregistration amendment or a separately-scoped bounded request, not just an approval — and its premise (that some earlier hour carries a usable snapshot) is an unverified hypothesis, since only hour 12 was checked and it was delta-only. The 0-vendor-call Lake certification above is unchanged and was independently reproduced bit-for-bit on this branch.
